title: chenopodium glaucum subsp. euglaucum
intro: Chenopodium glaucum subsp. euglaucum, also known as common orach, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium glaucum plant, a type of goosefoot or orach plant that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It is a perennial herb with edible leaves and seeds, which have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European and Middle Eastern medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Chenopodium glaucum subsp. euglaucum can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as be consumed as a fresh vegetable or used as an ingredient in salads and other dishes.
